---
title: "nitrix.stats.hgp"
generated: true
signature-hash: c99a13fa70b5ca63
description: "Mass-univariate **hierarchical** Gaussian-process regression (multi-level GP)."
listing:
  id: nitrix-stats-hgp-symbols
  contents: "nitrix-stats-hgp/*.qmd"
  type: table
  fields: [title, description]
  sort: "title"
  filter-ui: false
  page-size: 500
---

::: {.callout-important title="Generated reference stub — fill me"}
Auto-generated scaffold for `nitrix.stats.hgp`. Fill the prose *outside* the
generated markers; do **not** ship as-is (SPEC §6.5). `check_reference_staleness.py` flags signature drift.
:::

<!-- nitrix:gen overview:__module__ -->
Mass-univariate **hierarchical** Gaussian-process regression (multi-level GP).

`hgp_fit` fits, per element, a *hierarchical* GP smooth of a covariate `x`
over a grouping factor -- the GP analogue of a random-slope mixed model, and the
"GS" hierarchical GAM of Pedersen et al. (2019): a **global** smooth plus
**group-level** smooth deviations that share a kernel:
    y_ij = beta0 + f(x_ij) + f_{g(i)}(x_ij) + e_ij
    f      ~ GP(0, sigma_pop^2 K_rho)              (the population trend)
    f_g    ~ GP(0, sigma_grp^2 K_rho)  iid over g  (group deviations)
    e_ij   ~ N(0, sigma_e^2)

The group curves are *random deviations* around the population curve, sharing one
amplitude $\sigma_{grp}^2$ and the kernel lengthscale `rho` (so a group with few
observations is shrunk toward the population trend -- partial pooling).  This is
the multi-level / "(a)" scope of the GP feature: a hierarchical GP in the mixed-
model sense.
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

<!-- nitrix:gen dims:__page__ -->
**Tensor dimensions**

| dim | meaning |
|---|---|
| `V` | elements of a mass-univariate map — voxels, vertices, or fixels |
| `p` | fixed-effect coefficients / design-matrix columns |
| `K2` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `K` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
| `N` | observations — time points, subjects, or sampled points |
| `g` | *(undocumented — add to `_dimensions.yml`)* |
<!-- nitrix:/gen -->

## Symbols

::: {#nitrix-stats-hgp-symbols}
:::
